nodes.title: Store listeners in a plain object
nodes.why: A plain object gives a simple event-name to listener-array mapping.
nodes.alternative: Use a `Map` for explicit key separation.
nodes.tradeoff: Very familiar syntax, but object keys are string-based and can collide with inherited names in edge cases.

nodes.title: Create event list on first `on` call
nodes.why: This avoids pre-allocating lists for events that are never used.
nodes.alternative: Initialize all known events in constructor.
nodes.tradeoff: Memory-friendly and simple, but requires a guard each subscription.

nodes.title: Emit over a copied listener list
nodes.why: Copying prevents iteration issues if listeners unsubscribe while emit is running.
nodes.tradeoff: Safer behavior during mutation, with a small copy cost per emit.

nodes.title: Use `Map` for event registry
nodes.why: `Map` keeps keys isolated from object prototype behavior and works well for dynamic keys.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleaner key semantics, with slightly more verbose method calls.

nodes.title: Initialize list using `has`/`set`
nodes.why: This ensures every event has a listener array before pushing into it.
nodes.alternative: Read with `get`, then branch on undefined separately.
nodes.tradeoff: Clear control flow, but a bit more boilerplate than object access.

nodes.title: Emit from a snapshot of listeners
nodes.why: Listeners can remove themselves during emit; snapshot iteration keeps dispatch stable.
nodes.alternative: Loop over the original list directly.
nodes.tradeoff: More robust event dispatch, with minor per-emit allocation overhead.
